title: ANIP — Agent-Native Interface Protocol
description: Canonical JSON Schema for all ANIP v0.23 types as defined in SPEC.md. Use these schemas to validate ANIP documents, responses, and delegation tokens.

ServiceCapabilityRef.title: ServiceCapabilityRef
ServiceCapabilityRef.description: A reference to a capability on another service. Advisory only — not resolvable by the protocol. See SPEC.md §4.1.

Continuity.title: Continuity
Continuity.description: Continuity expectation for cross-service or recovery steps. v0.22 vocabulary: same_task. See SPEC.md §4.1.

CompletionMode.title: CompletionMode
CompletionMode.description: How completion is established for a cross-service contract entry. See SPEC.md §4.1.

target.description: The target capability on another service

required_for_task_completion.title: Required For Task Completion
required_for_task_completion.description: Whether this step is required for the logical task to be considered complete

continuity.description: Continuity expectation for this adjacent step

completion_mode.description: How completion is established for this adjacent step

CrossServiceContractEntry.title: CrossServiceContractEntry
CrossServiceContractEntry.description: A single entry in a cross-service contract — describes an adjacent step with task-local semantics. See SPEC.md §4.1.

handoff.title: Handoff
handoff.description: Adjacent capabilities that receive this capability's output for downstream processing

followup.title: Followup
followup.description: Adjacent capabilities for delayed or async status checking

verification.title: Verification
verification.description: Adjacent capabilities that verify this capability's side effects

CrossServiceContract.title: CrossServiceContract
CrossServiceContract.description: Structured cross-service contract with task-local adjacent-step semantics. Stronger than advisory cross_service hints. See SPEC.md §4.1.

RecoveryTargetKind.title: RecoveryTargetKind
RecoveryTargetKind.description: Kind of recovery step. See SPEC.md §4.5.

kind.description: What kind of recovery step this is

target.description: The specific capability to invoke for recovery. Omitted when recovery is local.

continuity.description: Continuity expectation for this recovery step

retry_after_target.title: Retry After Target
retry_after_target.description: Whether the original failed action should be retried after the recovery target succeeds

RecoveryTarget.title: RecoveryTarget
RecoveryTarget.description: Structured recovery target describing where and how recovery should proceed. See SPEC.md §4.5.

ConcurrentBranches.title: ConcurrentBranches
ConcurrentBranches.description: Whether concurrent delegation branches are allowed or exclusive. See SPEC.md §4.3.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Enforceable financial budget constraint in delegation tokens. See SPEC.md §4.3.

TokenRequest.description: Token issuance request. Root issuance omits parent_token; delegated issuance includes it.

subject.description: Principal identity the token is issued for.

parent_token.description: Token ID string of the parent token for delegated issuance (v0.22). NOT a JWT.

capability.description: Target capability this token is pre-bound to (v0.20).

budget.description: Budget constraint (v0.14). Must narrow relative to parent.

concurrent_branches.description: Concurrency posture for the token tree. Uses the same vocabulary as delegation constraints.

TokenResponse.description: Token issuance response. Returned by POST {tokens}.

issued.description: Whether the token was successfully issued.

token_id.description: Stable identifier for the issued token. Used as parent_token in delegated issuance.

task_id.description: Resolved purpose.task_id of the issued token. Present when the token has a resolved task identity; absent when no task identity was resolved.

budget.description: Echoed budget constraint when one was requested (v0.14).

budget_max.title: Budget Max
budget_max.description: The max_amount from the delegation token's budget constraint

budget_currency.title: Budget Currency
budget_currency.description: The currency from the delegation token's budget constraint

cost_check_amount.title: Cost Check Amount
cost_check_amount.description: The amount used for the pre-execution budget check

cost_certainty.description: The certainty level used in the budget check

cost_actual.title: Cost Actual
cost_actual.description: Actual cost after execution, null when not yet known or on pre-execution failure

within_budget.title: Within Budget
within_budget.description: Whether the actual cost was within the budget constraint

BudgetContext.title: BudgetContext
BudgetContext.description: Budget enforcement context included in invoke responses when a budget was evaluated. Present on both success and failure. See SPEC.md §6.3.

type.title: Type
type.description: Binding type identifier (e.g., quote, offer, price_lock)

field.title: Field
field.description: Which field in invocation parameters must carry the reference

source_capability.title: Source Capability
source_capability.description: Advisory — which capability produces this binding

max_age.title: Max Age
max_age.description: ISO 8601 duration — maximum age before binding is considered stale

BindingRequirement.title: BindingRequirement
BindingRequirement.description: Execution-time binding requirement for a capability. See SPEC.md §4.1.

ControlRequirementType.title: ControlRequirementType
ControlRequirementType.description: Type of control requirement. All types are token-evaluable: cost_ceiling, stronger_delegation_required. See SPEC.md §4.1.

ControlRequirementEnforcement.title: ControlRequirementEnforcement
ControlRequirementEnforcement.description: Enforcement mode for control requirements. v0.14 supports reject only. See SPEC.md §4.1.

ControlRequirement.title: ControlRequirement
ControlRequirement.description: Token-evaluable control requirement declaration for a capability. All control requirements can be checked from the delegation token alone. See SPEC.md §4.1.

budget.description: Enforceable financial budget constraint. Child budgets MUST NOT exceed parent. See SPEC.md §4.3.

DelegationConstraints.title: DelegationConstraints
DelegationConstraints.description: Constraints on delegation chain behavior. See SPEC.md §4.3.

Purpose.title: Purpose
Purpose.description: Purpose binding for a delegation token — which capability and task it authorizes. See SPEC.md §4.3.

EventClass.title: EventClass
EventClass.description: Classification of an audit event by risk level. See SPEC.md §6.8.

RetentionTier.title: RetentionTier
RetentionTier.description: Retention tier governing how long an audit entry is kept. See SPEC.md §6.8.

DisclosureLevel.title: DisclosureLevel
DisclosureLevel.description: How much failure detail is surfaced to callers. See SPEC.md §6.8.

ReasonType.title: ReasonType
ReasonType.description: Machine-readable classification of why a capability is restricted or denied in the permission response. See SPEC.md §4.4.

reason_type.description: Machine-readable classification of the denial. See SPEC.md §4.4.
Machine-readable classification of the restriction. See SPEC.md §4.4.

resolution_hint.title: Resolution Hint
resolution_hint.description: A canonical resolution.action value advising how this restriction can be resolved. MUST be consistent with the resolution.action that would appear in a failure if invocation were attempted. See SPEC.md §4.4 and §4.5.

unmet_token_requirements.title: Unmet Token Requirements
unmet_token_requirements.description: Token-evaluable control requirements not satisfied (e.g., cost_ceiling, stronger_delegation_required). See SPEC.md §4.4.

RecoveryClass.title: RecoveryClass
RecoveryClass.description: Coarse recovery strategy for a failure resolution. Advisory — does not override the retry boolean. See SPEC.md §4.5.

recovery_class.title: Recovery Class
recovery_class.description: Coarse recovery strategy implied by the action. Advisory — does not override retry. See SPEC.md §4.5.

recovery_target.title: Recovery Target
recovery_target.description: Structured recovery target (v0.22). Describes where and how recovery should proceed. Optional — additive to recovery_class. See SPEC.md §4.5.

DelegationToken.title: DelegationToken
DelegationToken.description: A delegation token representing scoped authority in a delegation chain (DAG). See SPEC.md §4.3.

approval_required.title: Approval Required
approval_required.description: Approval-required metadata. Present iff type='approval_required'. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.7.

ANIPFailure.title: ANIPFailure
ANIPFailure.description: Structured failure object with type, detail, resolution path, and retry hint. See SPEC.md §4.5.

minimum_scope.title: Minimum Scope
minimum_scope.description: Delegation scopes required to invoke this capability (AND semantics). Matches discovery document's minimum_scope field.

control_requirements.title: Control Requirements
control_requirements.description: Explicit control requirement declarations. See SPEC.md §4.1.

refresh_via.title: Refresh Via
refresh_via.description: Capability names (same manifest) that can refresh prerequisites or stale state

verify_via.title: Verify Via
verify_via.description: Capability names (same manifest) that can verify this capability's side effects

handoff_to.title: Handoff To
handoff_to.description: Capabilities on other services that are direct output consumers of this capability. Advisory only. See SPEC.md §4.1.

refresh_via.title: Cross-Service Refresh Via
refresh_via.description: Capabilities on other services that can refresh prerequisites or stale state before this capability is invoked. Advisory only. See SPEC.md §4.1.

verify_via.title: Cross-Service Verify Via
verify_via.description: Capabilities on other services that can verify this capability's side effects after invocation. Advisory only. See SPEC.md §4.1.

followup_via.title: Followup Via
followup_via.description: Capabilities on other services for delayed or async follow-up tasks spawned by this capability. Advisory only. See SPEC.md §4.1.

cross_service.title: Cross Service
cross_service.description: Advisory cross-service capability hints. Not enforced by the protocol. See SPEC.md §4.1.

cross_service_contract.title: Cross Service Contract
cross_service_contract.description: Structured cross-service contract with task-local adjacent-step semantics (v0.22). Stronger than advisory cross_service hints. See SPEC.md §4.1.

kind.description: Whether this capability is implemented atomically or as a declarative composition of other capabilities (v0.23). See SPEC.md §4.1 and §4.6.

composition.title: Composition
composition.description: Declarative composition metadata. Required when kind=composed. MUST be null/absent when kind=atomic. See SPEC.md §4.6.

grant_policy.title: Grant Policy
grant_policy.description: Policy controlling what approval grants MAY be issued for this capability when it raises approval_required. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.7.

allOf.// comment: Catches the omitted-kind case: if composition is non-null, kind MUST be 'composed'. The two above only fire when kind is explicitly present.

CapabilityDeclaration.title: CapabilityDeclaration
CapabilityDeclaration.description: Full capability declaration with inputs, outputs, side effects, cost, and observability. See SPEC.md §4.1.

PermissionResponse.title: PermissionResponse
PermissionResponse.description: Response from permission discovery — available, restricted, and denied capabilities. See SPEC.md §4.4.

budget.title: Budget
budget.description: Negotiation hint — MUST NOT widen the token's constraints.budget. See SPEC.md §6.3.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Caller-supplied task/workflow identity for grouping related invocations

parent_invocation_id.title: Parent Invocation Id
parent_invocation_id.description: Reference to the invocation that triggered this one

upstream_service.title: Upstream Service
upstream_service.description: Agent-supplied hint naming the originating service. Unvalidated. Echoed in response and recorded in audit. See SPEC.md §6.3.

approval_grant.title: Approval Grant
approval_grant.description: grant_id of an ApprovalGrant supplied on a continuation invocation after a prior approval_required failure. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.8.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Caller-supplied task/workflow identity for grouping related invocations

parent_invocation_id.title: Parent Invocation Id
parent_invocation_id.description: Reference to the invocation that triggered this one

budget_context.description: Budget enforcement context, present on both success and failure when a budget was evaluated. See SPEC.md §6.3.

upstream_service.title: Upstream Service
upstream_service.description: Echoed from request. Agent-supplied hint naming the originating service. See SPEC.md §6.3.

InvokeResponse.title: InvokeResponse
InvokeResponse.description: Response from capability invocation — result or failure with optional cost actual. See SPEC.md §6.2.

ANIPManifest.title: ANIPManifest
ANIPManifest.description: The full ANIP manifest — protocol version, profile versions, and all capability declarations. See SPEC.md §6.2.

task_id.title: Task ID
task_id.description: Task/workflow identity for grouping related invocations

parent_invocation_id.title: Parent Invocation ID
parent_invocation_id.description: Reference to the invocation that triggered this one

upstream_service.title: Upstream Service
upstream_service.description: Agent-supplied hint naming the originating service. Echoed from invoke request. See SPEC.md §6.3.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: When this entry will be deleted by retention enforcement, or null for indefinite retention

storage_redacted.title: Storage Redacted
storage_redacted.description: Whether this entry has been redacted during storage (e.g., detail stripped for aggregate_only tier)

entry_type.title: Entry Type
entry_type.description: Whether this is an individual or aggregated audit entry

aggregation_count.title: Aggregation Count
aggregation_count.description: Number of events aggregated into this entry, present only when entry_type is 'aggregated'

first_seen.title: First Seen
first_seen.description: Timestamp of the first event in an aggregated entry

last_seen.title: Last Seen
last_seen.description: Timestamp of the last event in an aggregated entry

representative_detail.title: Representative Detail
representative_detail.description: Representative detail string from one of the aggregated events

AuditEntry.title: AuditEntry
AuditEntry.description: An entry in the audit log with v0.9 classification, retention, and aggregation fields. See SPEC.md §6.5 and §6.8.

entry_type.title: Entry Type
entry_type.description: Discriminator indicating this is an aggregated entry

event_class.title: Event Class
event_class.description: Aggregated entries are always repeated_low_value_denial class

actor_key.title: Actor Key
actor_key.description: Identifier for the actor (e.g., delegation token subject)

grouping_key.title: Grouping Key
grouping_key.description: The key used to group individual events into this aggregate

window.title: Window
window.description: The time window over which events were aggregated

count.title: Count
count.description: Number of events in this aggregate

first_seen.title: First Seen
first_seen.description: Timestamp of the first event in the aggregate

last_seen.title: Last Seen
last_seen.description: Timestamp of the last event in the aggregate

representative_detail.title: Representative Detail
representative_detail.description: Representative detail string from one of the aggregated events, or null

AggregatedAuditEntry.title: AggregatedAuditEntry
AggregatedAuditEntry.description: An aggregated audit entry combining multiple repeated low-value denials. See SPEC.md §6.8.

proof_unavailable.title: Proof Unavailable
proof_unavailable.description: Present when proof cannot be generated, e.g. 'audit_entries_expired' when retention has deleted entries needed for proof. The checkpoint data is still valid.

expires_hint.title: Expires Hint
expires_hint.description: Best-effort ISO 8601 timestamp of earliest expected audit entry expiration in this checkpoint's range. Informational, not contractual. Not part of the signed checkpoint body.

CheckpointResponse.title: CheckpointResponse
CheckpointResponse.description: A Merkle checkpoint with optional proof. proof_unavailable indicates why proof generation failed. expires_hint is best-effort, informational. See SPEC.md §6.6 and §6.8.

CapabilityKind.title: CapabilityKind
CapabilityKind.description: Whether a capability is implemented atomically or as a declarative composition (v0.23). See SPEC.md §4.1.

AuthorityBoundary.title: AuthorityBoundary
AuthorityBoundary.description: Where composed steps run. Only same_service is supported in v0.23; same_package and external_service are reserved enum values that runtimes MUST reject.

EmptyResultPolicy.title: EmptyResultPolicy
EmptyResultPolicy.description: Policy for handling an empty intermediate result from a step flagged with empty_result_source. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.6.

FailurePolicyOutcome.title: FailurePolicyOutcome
FailurePolicyOutcome.description: How a child step's outcome is reflected in the parent capability response. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.6.

FailurePolicy.title: FailurePolicy
FailurePolicy.description: Failure outcome handling per child class. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.6.

AuditPolicy.title: AuditPolicy
AuditPolicy.description: Audit behavior for composed capabilities. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.6.

id.title: Step Id
id.description: Step identifier, unique within the composition.

capability.title: Capability
capability.description: The internal capability invoked at this step. MUST be kind=atomic when authority_boundary=same_service.

empty_result_source.title: Empty Result Source
empty_result_source.description: Flags this step's primary output as the trigger for empty_result_policy. At most one step per composition MAY set this to true.

empty_result_path.title: Empty Result Path
empty_result_path.description: Explicit JSONPath into this step's output used to detect emptiness when empty_result_source=true. If absent, the runtime uses the first downstream input_mapping reference into this step's output.

CompositionStep.title: CompositionStep
CompositionStep.description: An ordered step in a composed capability. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.6.

input_mapping.title: Input Mapping
input_mapping.description: step_id -> { param_name -> JSONPath }. JSONPath references resolve against $.input.* and earlier $.steps.<id>.output.*.

output_mapping.title: Output Mapping
output_mapping.description: parent_field -> JSONPath. Used when no empty-result branch is taken.

empty_result_output.title: Empty Result Output
empty_result_output.description: parent_field -> JSONPath ref or literal value. Used when the empty-result branch is taken with return_success_no_results. References must resolve only against $.input.* and the empty_result_source step's outputs.

Composition.title: Composition
Composition.description: Declarative composition metadata for kind=composed capabilities. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.6.

GrantType.title: GrantType
GrantType.description: Approval grant type. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.8.

ApprovalRequestStatus.title: ApprovalRequestStatus
ApprovalRequestStatus.description: Lifecycle status of an ApprovalRequest. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.7.

GrantPolicy.title: GrantPolicy
GrantPolicy.description: Constrains what an approver MAY issue for a given approval request. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.7.

ApprovalRequiredMetadata.title: ApprovalRequiredMetadata
ApprovalRequiredMetadata.description: Metadata attached to an approval_required failure response. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.7.

ApprovalRequest.title: ApprovalRequest
ApprovalRequest.description: Persistent record of a request for human/principal approval. Created when a capability raises approval_required. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.7.

ApprovalGrant.title: ApprovalGrant
ApprovalGrant.description: Signed authorization object issued after approval. Bound to capability, scope, parameter digest, requester, approver, expiry, and finite use count. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.8.

IssueApprovalGrantRequest.title: IssueApprovalGrantRequest
IssueApprovalGrantRequest.description: Request body for POST {approval_grants}. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.9.

IssueApprovalGrantResponse.title: IssueApprovalGrantResponse
IssueApprovalGrantResponse.description: Response body for POST {approval_grants}. v0.23. See SPEC.md §4.9.
